rawOcrText:
Consistency is the Ultimate
Catalyst for Recovery
Rehabilitation is a gradual process that rewards daily commitment.
Perform these movements within your comfort level and
focus on strict, controlled form.
If you experience sharp pain or sudden discomfort during any exercise, pause your routine and consult your Radiation Oncology or Cancer Specialist immediately.
A NotebookLM

rawOcrText:
Target Area: Head and Neck
Clinical Expectations
J — Week 3
Onset of mild mucositis (mouth ulcers) and
esophagitis (difficulty swallowing). Transition
- to semi-solids and 2.5L liquids daily.
Weeks 4-6
Mucositis and dysphagia become progressive.
—— Fluid intake is critical. Nutrition supplements
will be added by your care team.
Specific Rules
A No brushing teeth and no shaving the
A A face/neck until explicitly instructed.

rawOcrText:
Target Area: Breast and Thorax Clinical Expectations
Internal Symptoms
- The Timeline: Week 3 brings mild skin discoloration and a burning
( sensation in the throat (esophagitis). By Week 4, throat burning becomes
moderate to progressive, requiring a strict liquid/semi-solid diet.
/ Thorax Rule: Strictly avoid solid foods throughout the entire
radiation course.
External Skin Care
= Topical Defense Regimen:
+ Apply Cavilon Spray or Radvera Lotion at bedtime.
+ Apply Zolvera, Radvera, Radzinik, or Addcure lotion
5-6 times daily post-radiation.
A NotebookLM

/ Thorax Rule: Strictly avoid solid foods throughout the entire
radiation course.
External Skin Care
= Topical Defense Regimen:
+ Apply Cavilon Spray or Radvera Lotion at bedtime.
+ Apply Zolvera, Radvera, Radzinik, or Addcure lotion
5-6 times daily post-radiation.

rawOcrText:
Target Area: Abdomen and Pelvis
Clinical Expectations
Gastrointestinal Rules:
Avoid eating for at least 2 hours before
and after radiation to prevent nausea.
Maintain regular, free bowel movements to
avoid constipation.
r ¢
Weeks 3-6 Symptoms:
Progressive onset of diarrhea, bladder
- irritation (frequent, painful urination), and
- A) FLUIDS 2 MEDICATIONS rectal/anal irritation (radiation proctitis).
———
Action Plan:
Never hold urine for long durations. Use
prescribed medications (Imodium/Lomotil)
immediately to manage progressive diarrhea.
A NotebookLM
proposedText:
Target Area: Abdomen and Pelvis
Clinical Expectations
Gastrointestinal Rules:
Avoid eating for at least 2 hours before
and after radiation to prevent nausea.
Maintain regular, free bowel movements to
avoid constipation.
r ¢
Weeks 3-6 Symptoms:
Progressive onset of diarrhea, bladder
- irritation (frequent, painful urination), and
- A) FLUIDS 2 MEDICATIONS rectal/anal irritation (radiation proctitis).
Action Plan:
Never hold urine for long durations. Use
prescribed medications (Imodium/Lomotil)
immediately to manage progressive diarrhea.
notes: Auto-approved OCR from PNG image; review before clinical use.

Universal Care Foundations During Radiation
Do This Avoid This
Hydrate Relentlessly a hee aston
! . . lever apply soap on the radiation site
Drink at least 2.5 to 3 Litres of fluids (especially critical for Head, Neck, and
every day. Breast).
Pace Your Digestion No Unprescribed Lotions
Have small quantity meals at 2-3 hour Do not apply any oils or lotions without
intervals. direct doctor advice.
No Shaving or Trimming
Avoid shaving the treated region (beard
for Head/Neck; axilla/chest for
Thorax/Breast; pubic hair for Pelvis).
